reviews.review_msg: Two courses with Paul before he retired. Best: Paul picks good reads for the course! You go to Paul's course not thinking about the grade. You go to it because you know that English studies are about interdisciplinary and critical disposition and tons of reading. Honours effort, ideas and passion for the text!

He cares about his students and wants them to succeed. He goes beyond the typical teaching roles of a professor and tries to make real, personal connections with all of his students. What I liked the most from his class is that he encouraged us to be creative. As someone who dislikes formal essay writing, I enjoyed doing the assignments.

Paul is very caring, passionate, and inspirational. He is not the conventional professor; of course he wants you to develop into a good student, but he also wants you to develop into a kind person. He allows for maximum creativity and encourages you to take risks in order to learn something about yourself. Highly recommend.

SUCH A GOOD GUY!!!! I love him, he's so kind, caring, and intelligent. He is very open minded, and is always willing to lend a helping hand. He's truly one of the best profs that SFU has and I wish I could take more classes with him! Highly highly recommend!

Paul is a genuinely one of the sweetest professors I've had. He cares deeply about teaching and allows for students to explore literary works through creative projects instead of essays. He gives detailed feedback on graded work. He is a fair marker, and genuinely cares about seeing his students succeed. Take a class with him before he retires!!

One of the only professors who has the ability to leave an everlasting impression. He creates a safe learning environment. He allows you to truly be yourself & that in itself is refreshing. The students who are commenting on his "pointless" stories clearly did not understand that each anecdote served a purpose and related to the lecture. He's a gem

Paul is a great teacher! He is willing to put in the work if you are. He will provide you feedback on your essays and builds an atmosphere to promote teaching opposed to the standard "getting through the curriculum" many teachers offer.  English was never my strong suit, but Paul made me feel confident to continue writing. HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.

After taking this course, I became a huge fan of him. His lectures may come off as radical at times but his ideas are very inspirational and thought-provoking. And you can literally see he's really passionate about teaching and don't be afraid to take advantage of his office hours. He's very accommodating and provides in-depth feedback.

Paul is passionate and genuinely seems to enjoy teaching. He is helpful a sense that he offers extensive feedback on essays and assignments to help you do better next time. He is also an easy marker, oh, and there's no need to attend every class, participation matters but you'll get  a decent mark without going to every class.

Paul is eclectic. His passion for the topic is clear. For students however, the material is difficult and complicated to process, let alone write about. Little room for personal interpretation considering it's his lectures, reading his textbook. Very helpful nonetheless. TA's are not willing to mark easier, but still use them for guidance.
